description: Model-generated text is not a data source for deterministic facts; only tool, runner, filesystem, git, SQL, or log output can verify facts.
body: LLM text is a probabilistic claim. It may be useful for explanation, but it is not a source of truth for deterministic facts such as counts, test status, changed files, database rows, configuration values, or current runtime state. Verified facts must come from a tool or runner result. This fact applies to all models, including MoE and dense models. The correct fallback for missing evidence is to label the answer unverified, not to increase formatting strictness or add extra model calls. Use when a read-only query, count, list, status, diff, or inspection request must be answered from repository or tool evidence. Do not use when the user asks to edit, fix, deploy, migrate, delete, insert, update, or alter state. Actions: identify the exact scope, run read-only search or inspection tools, cite the source artifact, and preserve the command or file evidence used for the answer. Validation signals include matching counts against tool output, source references, scope labels, and no modified working-tree files. Risk boundary: stop or ask for confirmation if the task changes from query to mutation, if evidence is stale, or if the answer would expose secrets or excessive output.
